content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all four amendments against the actual code in model.rs and fs.rs. Three amendments identify genuine code quality and correctness issues worth addressing. One amendment (legacy collapse comments) is valid but the ask is modest — documentation rather than a code change. The migration amendment identifies a real operational risk for in-flight legacy rows during upgrade.

## Votes

- `fr-20-2f99f9e1`: Accept
  Confirmed: both `find_matching_running_task_run` (model.rs:462) and `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:480) accept `_started_at: DateTime<Utc>` but never use it — matching is solely by run_id via `entry.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id)`. The parameter is passed from call sites in fs.rs (lines 2816-2822, 2849-2855). Dead parameters in public functions are a real maintenance trap: a developer seeing `started_at` in the signature will reasonably assume it participates in matching and may waste time debugging timing-related issues. Removing it is a clean, low-risk improvement.
- `fr-20-33568ff5`: Accept
  Confirmed: `find_collapse_group_index` (model.rs:516-530) falls through to `unique_open_legacy_group_index` when `entry.run_id` is None, and `legacy_group_accepts_completion` (model.rs:564-573) matches terminal entries to open legacy starts by `started_at`. This is a read-only backward-compat path used when collapsing persisted entries during reads — it does not write new legacy data. If the acceptance criteria state 'all runless matching/backfill code is removed,' this surviving code needs at minimum a prominent comment block explaining why it was deliberately retained (read-only collapse of pre-existing legacy data). Without annotation, every future reviewer will flag the same apparent gap. The amendment's option (a) — adding documentation — is the right call since legacy data may still exist on disk.
- `fr-20-6b16d921`: Accept
  Confirmed: in `update_task_run` (fs.rs ~line 2962-2969), when no exact run_id match is found, open_matches are collected WITHOUT run_id filtering. For the single-match arm with a different run_id, an error is generated mentioning the mismatch. But the multi-open-match arm (the `_` arm) only says the match is 'ambiguous' without listing the run_ids of the conflicting entries. Adding the mismatched run_id(s) to that error message is a straightforward debuggability improvement — when operators hit this path, they need to know which run_ids are involved to diagnose the state. Low risk, high diagnostic value.
- `fr-20-dfd9659f`: Accept
  Confirmed: `find_matching_running_task_run` and `matching_finalized_task_runs` now require `entry.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id)`, so legacy rows with `run_id: None` are invisible to update_task_run's exact-match path. If a legacy running row exists and a new start arrives, `fail_superseded_running_attempt` (fs.rs:2702-2723) marks it Failed — meaning the original work is silently discarded as 'superseded by retry' even though it was legitimately in progress. There is no migration or backfill step to assign run_ids to pre-existing running rows. The collapse_task_run_attempts pipeline handles merging during reads, but that doesn't help the write path in update_task_run which needs an exact run_id match. This is a real operational risk during upgrade: any milestone started under the old build can never complete normally. A one-time migration that assigns run_ids to open legacy rows (or at minimum documents the upgrade constraint) is warranted.
